content.text:
Confirmed — and it changes the picture in your favor. Two things first: I was wrong to call JJK a domain mismatch, and you're right that the officium is the bind. But it's better than even that.

## The attribution data is already in-band

I worried the hard part would be joining transcript turns to heats — a fuzzy `sessionId → officium → coronet` correlation across files, made worse by your concurrent officia. That worry is moot. **Every jjx call is recorded in the transcript as a `tool_use`, carrying its coronet/firemark *and* its officium**, interleaved with the billed turns, in timestamp order, in the same file. The grep above shows it plainly: `jjx_record ₢A6AAC` under officium `☉260413-1019`, sitting right next to the turns it cost.

So attribution is a **single forward pass over one session's transcript** — maintain "current pace" from the most recent coronet-bearing jjx call, attribute each billed turn to it. No external join, no `sessionId↔officium` puzzle, and concurrency is a non-issue because each transcript self-describes. That's the thing that makes JJK not merely an acceptable home but the *correct* one: the cost data and the work-context are already co-located, and JJK owns the vocabulary (coronet/firemark/officium) that reads them.

## Rust + multiformat: yes, and for a concrete reason

This isn't speculative future-proofing — the schema has *already* drifted under you. `costUSD` existed in older Claude Code and is gone; `usage` has grown `iterations`, the `cache_creation` 5m/1h split, `service_tier`. serde with optional fields and a normalization layer (`RawRecord → BilledTurn { model, usage, id, session, ts, pace? }`) absorbs that drift for free, and a `trait TranscriptSource` lets a second impl read claude.ai web exports later (your WCC lens already anticipates that boundary). It also gives the rate table one authoritative home — which matters here, because the bash prototype and a Rust engine must not both carry a price table. So: Rust wins, and the `/tmp` prototype is now throwaway — it did its job (proved the method, produced the $6,839).

## The design fork I'd actually want your judgment on

The markers aren't symmetric, and your workflow rhythm decides how to read them:

- **`jjx_orient` (mount)** is a *prospective* marker — "I'm now starting heat X." It opens a span going forward.
- **`jjx_record` / `jjx_close` (notch/wrap)** are *retrospective* — you do the work, *then* notch it. The marker sits at the *end* of the span it describes.

So naive "attribute to the last coronet seen" misfiles the work that preceded its own notch. The accurate model is span-aware (orient opens forward, notch closes backward). But how cleanly that holds depends on your actual cadence — do you mount before working, or notch as you go? That's yours to tell me, not mine to assume.

Two more in a line each: there'll be an honest **unattributed bucket** (turns before the first jjx call in a session, or sessions that never touched JJK — including all pre-JJK chats); and **transcript retention bounds the past** — `~/.claude` runs a cleanup (`.last-cleanup` is right there), so "study old chats" reaches only as far back as Claude Code has retained, which I'd want to check before promising depth.

That's my read. The richest thread is the marker-directionality one — want to pull on that, or should I trot the whole design (engine shape, command surface, the mint it'll need) in counted chunks?

content.text: This reframes things, and it's the right instinct — but "capture chats" hides two different goals with opposite bloat profiles. Let me get real numbers before I answer, because the bloat question turns entirely on *what* you capture.

input.command:

echo "=== raw transcript footprint (what 'capture everything' would commit) ==="
du -sh "$DIR" 2>/dev/null
du -sh ~/.claude/projects 2>/dev/null | sed 's/$/   (all projects)/'
echo
echo "=== biggest single session file ==="
ls -S "$DIR"/*.jsonl | head -1 | xargs ls -lh | awk '{print $5, $NF}'
echo
echo "=== git-hostility probe: longest single line in the biggest file (embedded blobs?) ==="
BIG=$(ls -S "$DIR"/*.jsonl | head -1)
awk '{ if (length > m) m = length } END { print m " bytes (max line)" }' "$BIG"
echo
echo "=== cost-projection size: usage slice only, all history ==="
RAW=$(jq -c 'select(.type=="assistant")|{s:.sessionId,i:.message.id,m:.message.model,u:.message.usage,ts:.timestamp}' "$DIR"/*.jsonl 2>/dev/null | tee /tmp/proj.ndjson | wc -c)
GZ=$(gzip -c /tmp/proj.ndjson | wc -c)
printf 'projection raw:      %s bytes (%.1f MB)\n' "$RAW" "$(echo "$RAW/1048576" | bc -l)"
printf 'projection gzipped:  %s bytes (%.1f MB)  <- git footprint\n' "$GZ" "$(echo "$GZ/1048576" | bc -l)"

content.text:
Here's the number that answers it: **839 MB** of raw transcript for this project alone (≈13 MB/day and climbing), versus **3 MB gzipped for the entire cost-relevant projection across all of history.** That ~280× gap is the whole answer — bloat depends entirely on *what* you capture, and "capture chats" is quietly two different proposals.

## Should you worry? Split the goal first

- **Capturing the accounting substrate** (the `usage` slice + the jjx `tool_use` markers, dropping prose/file-reads/pastes): 3 MB gzipped for everything you've ever done here, tens of KB/day going forward. **No, never worry.** This compresses to nothing and diffs cleanly because it's append-only.
- **Capturing the full chats** (the actual conversations): 839 MB now, +13 MB/day. And the bytes are the *smaller* half of the problem — see below. **Yes, worry** — but not mainly about size.

## Capture is the right instinct — for durability

Your move solves the two limits I flagged last turn for free: `~/.claude` is GC-pruned (that `.last-cleanup` will eventually eat your old chats) and it's machine-local (cerebro, winpc, here — each has a partial view). An append-only projection committed to git is **GC-proof, reproducible, and multi-machine-merged** — each machine sweeps its own captures into one durable record. The `jjx_expenses` command then reconstructs from committed history with zero `~/.claude` dependency at study time. That's strictly better than live-reading, and it's a genuinely good idea.

And it has a natural home: JJK already commits its state into `.claude/jjm/` (the gallops), which is consumer-local and therefore *already outside* what the kit-parceling distributes. A per-session projection ledger living right there inherits that exclusion. The capture hook is even cleaner than mount/groom — fold it into the commands that *already* self-commit (notch/wrap): every jjx commit also sweeps the session's new turns into the ledger.

## The thing the musing may not have front-of-mind

This repo is a **distribution source** — `cma-prep-pr`, `rbk-prep-release`, `OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM`. Full chats contain veiled files, the bhyslop-specific material, raw design talk. Committing *those* into this repo makes the parceling exclusion load-bearing for privacy, not just hygiene — one leak and a design conversation rides into a public PR. The projection is metadata (token counts, model ids, coronets, timestamps — no prose), so it's far safer *and* lands in the already-excluded `.claude/jjm/`. If you also want the real conversations preserved as conversations, that wants a *separate* store (a private chat-vault repo), never this one. One line, happy to expand.

## "Fix JJK on format change" — that's your Palisade, and it's right

Claude Code's format is ungovernable (vendor-controlled, changes without notice) — exactly the verdict-less boundary your ROE describes. "Fix it when it drifts" is correct Palisade conduct. Two refinements make it safe:
1. **Capture must fail *loud* on an unrecognized shape**, never silently skip — silent skips become silent undercounts, and you'd never know to fix JJK.
2. **Capture generously enough to re-interpret after the fix.** If you store a *minimal* projection and the format moves a field, you discarded it before you knew. Storing the full `usage` object + jjx markers verbatim (just not the prose) keeps re-interpretation possible while still shedding the 839 MB. Capture raw-ish, interpret at study time.

So my read: capture the generous projection into `.claude/jjm/`, leave the full chats out of this repo, and you get GC-proof per-heat accounting with no bloat worth the name. The full-chat archive is a real but separate want.

Want me to trot the concrete shape next — ledger format, the notch/wrap capture hook, the parse-at-study-time split — or keep pulling on the archive-vs-projection fork?

content.text: Good — the veiled path solves the leak cleanly; now it's purely a size question. And "is the chat worth its bytes" is empirical, so let me find where the 839 MB actually lives before naming levers.

input.content:
# Where do the transcript bytes live? Content-proportion breakdown (string lengths,
# a proxy for bytes — good enough for 80/20). Streams via reduce to stay memory-light.
reduce inputs as $r (
  { seen:{}, recs:0, urecs:0,
    tr:0, atext:0, think:0, tuse:0, utext:0, other:0 };
  .recs += 1
  | ( ($r.uuid // null) as $u
      | if ($u != null) and (.seen[$u] == true)
        then .
        else (if $u != null then .seen[$u] = true else . end) | .urecs += 1
        end )
  | if $r.type == "assistant" then
      .atext += ([ $r.message.content[]? | select(.type=="text")     | (.text     // "") | length ] | add // 0)
      | .think += ([ $r.message.content[]? | select(.type=="thinking") | (.thinking // "") | length ] | add // 0)
      | .tuse  += ([ $r.message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use")  | (tojson | length) ] | add // 0)
    elif $r.type == "user" then
      ( ($r.message.content) as $c
        | if ($c | type) == "array" then
            .tr    += ([ $c[]? | select(.type=="tool_result") | (.content | tojson | length) ] | add // 0)
            | .utext += ([ $c[]? | select(.type=="text") | (.text // "") | length ] | add // 0)
          else
            .utext += (($c // "") | tostring | length)
          end )
    else
      .other += ($r | tojson | length)
    end
)
| { records: .recs,
    unique_by_uuid: .urecs,
    dup_record_pct: ((100 * (.recs - .urecs) / .recs) | floor),
    content_MB: {
      tool_result:    ((.tr    / 1048576) | floor),
      assistant_text: ((.atext / 1048576) | floor),
      thinking:       ((.think / 1048576) | floor),
      tool_use:       ((.tuse  / 1048576) | floor),
      user_text:      ((.utext / 1048576) | floor),
      other_records:  ((.other / 1048576) | floor)
    } }

content.text:
Measured. The breakdown is decisive — and it corrects something I said earlier.

| Content bucket | ~MB | What it is |
|---|---|---|
| **tool_result** | **102** | file reads, grep/glob output, command/test output |
| tool_use | 26 | tool inputs — incl. full Write/Edit payloads |
| other_records | 18 | per-session injected context (the CLAUDE.md + @-includes boilerplate) |
| **assistant_text** | **16** | the assistant's actual prose |
| **user_text** | **4** | your messages |
| thinking | ~0 | omitted/empty on 4.8/4.7 |
| embedded images | 0 | none — the 150 KB max line is a file read, not a blob |

**The chat you actually want to study is the prose: ~20 MB for all of history.** The other ~146 MB of content (and most of the structural inflation up to 839 MB) is *machinery* — and here's the thesis: **almost all of it is recoverable from git or compressible, because it's the agent reading and writing files that are already in your repo.**

## The 80/20, ranked by real impact

1. **Strip tool_result content for git-recoverable reads** (~102 MB, the giant). A `Read`/`Grep`/`Glob` of a committed file stores the file's *content* inline — but you have that content in git at that commit. Keep the call + path + commit ref, drop the bytes: **lossless given the repo.** Keep `Bash`/`WebFetch` results verbatim (those aren't in git). This one lever does most of the work.
2. **Strip Write/Edit payloads from tool_use** (part of the 26 MB). The `new_string`/`content` being written *is* what landed in git. Same trick: keep the fact + ref, drop the bytes.
3. **Drop the re-injected context boilerplate** (~18 MB). The CLAUDE.md + @-includes wall is re-stamped every session and it's literally your repo's own files — reference, don't store.
4. **Let git gc handle fork redundancy — don't hand-dedup.** Correction to what I said earlier: ~49% of *assistant* records are forked/resumed copies (same API `message.id`), but the fork re-stamps every record's `uuid`, so there's no clean uuid key to dedup on. The good news — those copies are *verbatim*, so git's packfile delta-compression collapses them on its own. (Dedup-by-`message.id` still matters, but only in the cost *parser*, to avoid double-billing — not in archive storage.)

Net: keeping the conversation while referencing-instead-of-restoring takes you from 839 MB toward the **~20–50 MB** that's genuinely irreducible, and it *packs* to single-digit MB.

## The clean way to make it tunable — fidelity levels

Rather than one filter, capture at a chosen fidelity and let the veiled archive sit at the sweet spot:

- **L0 — accounting:** usage projection only. ~3 MB gzipped, all history. (cost study reads this)
- **L1 — readable chat:** prose + tool *calls/args* + decisions, tool *results* referenced-not-stored. ~single-digit MB packed. **This is "study old chats" — the whole conversation, readable, tiny.**
- **L2 — replayable:** L1 + capped tool_result content + kept command output. Tens of MB.
- **L3 — full raw:** 839 MB. Cold storage, out of the repo.

I'd put the veiled archive at **L1**, with L0 folded in. The only loss at L1 is the *content* of git-recoverable reads — and "fix later" just means re-deriving from the repo at that commit, so it's not really lost.

One honest caveat: L1 stripping *is* irreversible at capture for the truly ad-hoc stuff (a `Bash` test run's output, a `WebFetch`) — so the rule is "strip what git holds, keep what it doesn't." That keeps L1 both tiny and complete-where-it-counts.

message.content: I made a jump I think that you haven't: do you agree we will be doing this in job jockey?  Do you assess the compression tools available in bash have safer provenance?

content.text:
Two good catches, and you're right on both — including one where I need to correct myself.

**Yes — JJK/Rust is the home, decided.** You're right that I lagged. The bash and the `~/.claude` poking has been my measuring scratchpad — proving ratios and record shapes to inform the design — not a candidate implementation. The deliverable is Rust in `jjx`: read transcripts → projection (cost) + the veiled L1 archive, parse-at-study-time. I shouldn't have kept reaching for bash as if it were still in contention; it's done its job (the $6,839, the 102 MB tool_result finding, the 20 MB-of-prose finding). I'll stop treating it as a home.

**On provenance: no — and I shouldn't have implied the bash tools were safer last turn.** That was a conflation. "Fewer crates in my dependency tree" is not the same as "safer provenance," and for *your* premise it's arguably backwards:

- A system `zstd` CLI is an **ambient, unpinned** binary — whatever Homebrew put here, apt put on cerebro, something else on winpc. The repo can't pin it, can't checksum it, can't record which one ran, and it's PATH-hijackable. For "tightly manage our exposure," that's the *harder* thing to govern, not the easier one.
- A Cargo-locked crate is **pinned + checksummed + `cargo-vet`/`cargo-deny`/`cargo-audit`-able** — the exact provenance becomes committed, reviewable repo state. That *is* managed exposure.
- And the punchline: it's the **same libzstd C underneath either way**. The compression engine's provenance is identical; the only difference is whether *you* control and record it (crate) or trust whatever the machine happens to have (CLI). The CLI also drags us back into the bash idiom we just agreed to leave.

So the appeal of shelling out was ergonomics, not safety — I mislabeled it.

**Where that leaves the recommendation, ranked by your values (manage exposure → prefer pinned/minimal):**

1. **Add nothing — let git's own zlib + delta compress the committed text.** git is already your most-trusted dependency (the repo *is* git). Forked-copy redundancy deltas to near-nothing; JSON text zlib-compresses ~4–5×; zero new crate, zero new binary, zero new `build.rs`. This is the tightest possible exposure.
2. **Pure-Rust compressor** if you want more in-engine control without C — `miniz_oxide`/`flate2`'s Rust backend (zlib-class, no C FFI), pinned and vet-able.
3. **The `zstd` crate** (C libzstd via FFI, `build.rs` compiles it) — best ratio, still pinned/checksummed/vet-able, but it's the only one adding a C + unsafe + build-time-codegen surface. Worth it *only if* a measured ratio gain justifies that surface.
4. **Shell to system `zstd`** — avoid. Best ratio, worst-managed provenance, off-architecture.

And this dissolves your filtering waffle nicely: if git compresses the committed archive for free, full-fidelity capture (skip the tool-exclusion logic, keep the whole chat for rare spelunking) gets *cheap* without any new dependency — so leaning away from filtering is fine.

The one open empirical question is whether `zstd`'s ratio over git's zlib is large enough to earn that C surface, given the data is already git-delta-friendly and rarely read. I'd bet "not worth it" — but I can measure git-pack-vs-zstd on your real transcripts to settle it whenever you want. Want that number, or are we good landing on "let git do it"?

message.content: You framed the case beautifully; balance of interests heavily favors embracing git's own compression.

content.text:
Agreed — git is the compressor, and we add nothing to the trust surface to get it. That's the rare decision where the safe choice and the simple choice are the same one: full-fidelity capture stays cheap, provenance stays exactly as managed as your repo already is, and there's no new crate, binary, or `build.rs` for a nefarious actor to aim at.

Worth noting, lightly: every decision from this thread lives only in this chat right now — which is precisely the evaporation the feature exists to prevent. So we've reached a natural consolidation point. Here's the shape we've actually cinched:

- **Home:** JJK / Rust (`jjx`), reading Claude Code transcripts.
- **Two outputs:** per-heat/pace cost accounting + a veiled full-fidelity chat archive (consumer-local, outside kit distribution).
- **Attribution:** in-band — the jjx `tool_use` records in each transcript mark which heat/pace was active; no external join, concurrency-safe.
- **Cost dedup:** by API `message.id` in the parser (forked copies bill once); git delta handles the storage redundancy.
- **Compression:** git's own zlib + delta. Nothing added.
- **Fidelity:** capture full, skip tool-exclusion filtering (git makes it affordable).
- **Format drift:** Palisade posture — fail loud on unrecognized shapes, fix JJK when Claude Code's format moves, capture raw-ish so re-interpretation stays possible.

Still genuinely open — and the richest is one you haven't weighed in on yet:

- **Marker directionality** (the one I raised, then we detoured around): `orient`/mount opens a span *forward*, `notch`/`wrap` closes one *backward*. Which dominates depends on your actual rhythm — do you mount before working a pace, or work-then-notch? That decision shapes the attribution engine.
- **Capture trigger** (fold into the self-committing notch/wrap? a sweep at mount/groom?) and **retention race** (capture must outrun `~/.claude`'s GC).
- The command's **mint** (the `jjx_expenses` placeholder needs a real word) — your call, when we get there.

So: pull the directionality thread next, or would you rather I consolidate this into something durable — a memo, or the bones of a paddock — before we keep going?

message.content: Groom the BD heat because I think we are getting close to clarity that informs a pace.  In fact, I think the key thing today is to _not_ do the cost accounting, but focus on securing the chat logs now before they start silently disappearing.  We need techniques that never forget chat logs but do update.  I'm not at all concerned about 'slurping up' unrelated chats, these are kept as reference in our own system.  We can add cost and other analytics after.  The safest capture triggers are groom and mount; we do those more frequently than wrapping, because sometimes we daisy chain chats to finish a pace.

content.text:
You're right to cut scope, and right about the urgency: the logs are the perishable asset. Cost is reconstructable from captured logs at any later date — but you cannot analyze a chat that's already been silently GC'd. So **capture-first, analytics-later** is the correct sequencing, not a compromise. I've got the rest of the reframe too:

- **Wholesale, no filtering** — mirror every session in the project's transcript dir; relevance/attribution is a later concern, not a capture-time gate.
- **Never-forget but update** — additive sync: pick up new session files and the growth of existing ones, never delete from the store even when the source disappears. (Git history carries the "never forget"; the working store carries the live union.)
- **Triggers: mount and groom** — chosen because they fire more often than wrap, since you daisy-chain chats to finish a pace.

Let me groom ₣BD to see its current shape before we slate this. Opening the officium first (it has to land on its own), so this turn just loads the JJK tool.

### show-target-list-and-round-trip-bridge (₢BDAAS) [rough]

## Character
Design settled in conversation — mechanical implementation against cinched decisions plus a doc deliverable. Live read path: orient shares the gazette plumbing, so verify it survives.

## Goal
Make jjx_show the gazette round-trip surface: accept a heterogeneous target list (per-element length-dispatch), always populate the gazette with the resolved set while keeping the tool-result terse, and document the output->reslate bridge so a batch of dockets can be pulled, bash-edited, and replumbed as a mass reslate — without a new verb.

## Cinched
- MCP is the only client. Cut `detail` entirely: tool-result is always the terse table; gazette is always populated with paddock(s) + pace dockets. Bodies reach context only by Read-ing the gazette (the existing pattern); the round-trip never Reads it.
- `targets` is a list; each element self-types by length (firemark -> expand, coronet -> single, unconditional). Singular and no-target auto-select must keep working (orient/mount depend on auto-select).
- `remaining` is REQUIRED and affects firemark expansion only; coronet-named targets return regardless of state.
- Type wall holds: show still emits jjezs_pace (output-typed). The round-trip is a deliberate bash bridge (drop jjezs_paddock, rewrite jjezs_pace->jjezs_reslate, apply the edit) — no permissive parser, no new verb, nothing in jjx emits reslate-shaped output.
- No reslate dry-run; trust the per-coronet diff-echo, revisit only after 3 real collisions.
- No orient-ordering dependency (verified): jjx_open — not orient — creates the officium dir, and every command requires the officium id, so groom's show always finds it. But the now load-bearing gazette write is `.ok()`-swallowed today, so a missing officium dir (e.g. mid-session exsanguination) silently no-ops; decide at implementation whether the always-on gazette must crash-fast there rather than inherit that silence.
- Guidance deliverable (CLAUDE.md JJK gazette section): the canonical bridge recipe + the transformable-vs-bespoke "when to reach for it" boundary (transformable edits win; bespoke novel-prose authors per-pace).

## Test scenario
The round-trip's own origin: cross-heat, transformable heading renames — the `## Locked` -> `## Cinched` and `## Done` -> `## Done when` shapes this pace was born from — driven end-to-end (show heterogeneous targets -> bash bridge -> mass reslate), asserting faithful per-docket change. The fixture constructs its own dockets; it must not depend on any live un-migrated docket surviving to mount.

## Done when
show takes a heterogeneous target list, always populates the gazette, returns a terse result, and requires `remaining`; orient still works; the multi-heat case (bitmap/swimlanes assume a single heat) is handled or suppressed; CLAUDE.md documents the show->sed->reslate bridge and the transformable-vs-bespoke boundary; theurge/unit cover list dispatch and the always-on gazette; the test scenario above passes end-to-end.

## Entry points
jjrpd_parade.rs (target dispatch + gazette population), jjrz_gazette.rs (slug vocabulary + read-output builder).

### mount-groom-gazette-heat-selection (₢BDAAT) [rough]

## Character
Intricate but mechanical — server change plus spec/doc sync; the decisions are cinched, nothing left to design.

## Goal
Make `jjx_orient` (mount) and the heat-overview form of `jjx_show` (groom) take their heat firemark **solely from `gazette_in.md`**, never from params. Writing the gazette becomes a required first step of mount/groom, so the Claude Code `Write` permission fires at ceremony start instead of silently stalling a chat at the first later slate/reslate/paddock-set. Single-pace parade (`jjx_show {target: <coronet>}`) stays param-based.

## Cinched
- **Hard / sole input** — a param firemark on these two paths is *rejected*, not a fallback. Soft conventions slip unnoticed; that silent stall is exactly what this fixes.
- **Scope = mount + groom-form only.** Single-pace parade stays param-based and cheap.
- **Deliberate override of getter/setter orthodoxy** — the gazette normally carries content, not identities; here it carries a body-less firemark notice on purpose, to make the ceremony `Write` load-bearing. Operator decision; do not re-litigate.

## Done looks like
- New read-command input slug(s) minted (minting rules + Quoin discipline) and wired into the gazette parser; both paths require + parse the gazette firemark and reject a param firemark with a loud error.
- CLAUDE.md synced: Mount Protocol, Groom Protocol, and the Officium Protocol gazette tables/notes (input-slug table, getter/setter line).
- **JJS0 spec family updated** — the main spec plus every affected command spec and the gazette wire-format section. Discovery: `grep -rln 'jjx_orient\|jjx_show\|jjezs_' Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJS*.adoc`.
- Param callers of these two paths reconciled to write the gazette first (Hard enforcement breaks them). Discovery: grep the JJK crate + tests for orient/show invocations.

Mostly mechanical substitution, plus two small mint decisions requiring operator judgment.

### record-warning-decompose-rename-pairs (₢BDAAW) [rough]

`jjx_record`'s "uncommitted changes outside file list" warning treats a staged rename's
`old -> new` porcelain line as one opaque path,
so a rename whose old and new paths are BOTH in the `files[]` list still warns as outside the list.
Evidence: the ₣BH bash-filename-case sweep (commit 94729c007) listed both sides of all 50 `git mv` pairs,
yet every pair printed in the warning — 50 false lines drowning any real outside-list signal.
The commit itself was correct; the defect is matcher honesty, not staging.

## Character
Small mechanical Rust fix with a unit test; the only judgment call is the matching rule.

## Cinched
- Decompose `R`-status lines into their two paths before matching against the file list;
  a rename counts as covered when both sides are listed
  (whether new-path-only should also satisfy is a mount-time call — pick the stricter rule if unsure).

## Done when
- A `jjx_record` whose file list names both sides of a staged rename emits no outside-list warning for that pair.
- A rename genuinely absent from the file list still warns.
- A unit test covers the rename-pair case.

- `targets` is a list; each element self-types by length (firemark -> expand, coronet -> single, unconditional). Singular and no-target auto-select must keep working (orient/mount depend on auto-select).
- `remaining` is REQUIRED and affects firemark expansion only; coronet-named targets return regardless of state.
- Type wall holds: show still emits jjezs_pace (output-typed). The round-trip is a deliberate bash bridge (drop jjezs_paddock, rewrite jjezs_pace->jjezs_reslate, apply the edit) — no permissive parser, no new verb, nothing in jjx emits reslate-shaped output.
- No reslate dry-run; trust the per-coronet diff-echo, revisit only after 3 real collisions.
- No orient-ordering dependency (verified): jjx_open — not orient — creates the officium dir, and every command requires the officium id, so groom's show always finds it. But the now load-bearing gazette write is `.ok()`-swallowed today, so a missing officium dir (e.g. mid-session exsanguination) silently no-ops; decide at implementation whether the always-on gazette must crash-fast there rather than inherit that silence.
- Guidance deliverable (CLAUDE.md JJK gazette section): the canonical bridge recipe + the transformable-vs-bespoke "when to reach for it" boundary (transformable edits win; bespoke novel-prose authors per-pace).

## Test scenario
The round-trip's own origin: cross-heat, transformable heading renames — the `## Locked` -> `## Cinched` and `## Done` -> `## Done when` shapes this pace was born from — driven end-to-end (show heterogeneous targets -> bash bridge -> mass reslate), asserting faithful per-docket change. The fixture constructs its own dockets; it must not depend on any live un-migrated docket surviving to mount.

## Done when
show takes a heterogeneous target list, always populates the gazette, returns a terse result, and requires `remaining`; orient still works; the multi-heat case (bitmap/swimlanes assume a single heat) is handled or suppressed; CLAUDE.md documents the show->sed->reslate bridge and the transformable-vs-bespoke boundary; theurge/unit cover list dispatch and the always-on gazette; the test scenario above passes end-to-end.

## Entry points
jjrpd_parade.rs (target dispatch + gazette population), jjrz_gazette.rs (slug vocabulary + read-output builder).

### mount-groom-gazette-heat-selection (₢BDAAT) [rough]

## Character
Intricate but mechanical — server change plus spec/doc sync; the decisions are cinched, nothing left to design.

## Goal
Make `jjx_orient` (mount) and the heat-overview form of `jjx_show` (groom) take their heat firemark **solely from `gazette_in.md`**, never from params. Writing the gazette becomes a required first step of mount/groom, so the Claude Code `Write` permission fires at ceremony start instead of silently stalling a chat at the first later slate/reslate/paddock-set. Single-pace parade (`jjx_show {target: <coronet>}`) stays param-based.

## Cinched
- **Hard / sole input** — a param firemark on these two paths is *rejected*, not a fallback. Soft conventions slip unnoticed; that silent stall is exactly what this fixes.
- **Scope = mount + groom-form only.** Single-pace parade stays param-based and cheap.
- **Deliberate override of getter/setter orthodoxy** — the gazette normally carries content, not identities; here it carries a body-less firemark notice on purpose, to make the ceremony `Write` load-bearing. Operator decision; do not re-litigate.

## Done looks like
- New read-command input slug(s) minted (minting rules + Quoin discipline) and wired into the gazette parser; both paths require + parse the gazette firemark and reject a param firemark with a loud error.
- CLAUDE.md synced: Mount Protocol, Groom Protocol, and the Officium Protocol gazette tables/notes (input-slug table, getter/setter line).
- **JJS0 spec family updated** — the main spec plus every affected command spec and the gazette wire-format section. Discovery: `grep -rln 'jjx_orient\|jjx_show\|jjezs_' Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJS*.adoc`.
- Param callers of these two paths reconciled to write the gazette first (Hard enforcement breaks them). Discovery: grep the JJK crate + tests for orient/show invocations.

### jjs0-glossary-quoin-emphasis (₢BDAAV) [rough]

## Character
Mostly mechanical substitution, plus two small mint decisions requiring operator judgment.

In the Racing Vocabulary glossary of JJS0,
five upper-vocabulary verbs appear as bare italic mentions (*muster*, *parade*, *furlough*, *retire*, *rail*)
even though `jjsuv_` quoins with definition sites already exist;
replace each italic mention with its quoin ref,
matching how the same bullet list already renders the heat/pace/firemark/coronet/silks/gallops entries.
Discovery recipe: `grep -n '\*[a-z-]*\*' Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJS0_JobJockeySpec.adoc` —
the glossary-section hits are the targets.

Two glossary words have no concept quoin to point at:
*paddock* (the artifact concept — existing quoins cover only the path field and the jjx command)
and *docket* (the presentation alias — only the revise-docket operation quoins exist).
Resolve with the operator whether to mint concept quoins for these two;
if minted, follow MCM linked-term structure and the Quoin Sub-Letter Discipline.

Edits follow MCM line-break discipline wherever a line is restructured.

## Cinched
Dressage vocabulary (school, longe, breeze, corral, volte, caracole, gait, beat, quirt, reined) stays italic —
V4 reserved vocabulary with no machinery behind it; out of scope.
Run-in labels (e.g. *Invocation:*, *Design rationale:*) and literal-block output templates
(e.g. the JJSCRT trophy markdown) are correct as-is; do not touch.

## Done when
The five jjsuv glossary mentions are quoin refs,
the paddock/docket mint question is resolved with the operator (minted or explicitly declined),
and the document's attribute refs all resolve.

### record-warning-decompose-rename-pairs (₢BDAAW) [rough]

`jjx_record`'s "uncommitted changes outside file list" warning treats a staged rename's
`old -> new` porcelain line as one opaque path,
so a rename whose old and new paths are BOTH in the `files[]` list still warns as outside the list.
Evidence: the ₣BH bash-filename-case sweep (commit 94729c007) listed both sides of all 50 `git mv` pairs,
yet every pair printed in the warning — 50 false lines drowning any real outside-list signal.
The commit itself was correct; the defect is matcher honesty, not staging.

## Character
Small mechanical Rust fix with a unit test; the only judgment call is the matching rule.

## Cinched
- Decompose `R`-status lines into their two paths before matching against the file list;
  a rename counts as covered when both sides are listed
  (whether new-path-only should also satisfy is a mount-time call — pick the stricter rule if unsure).

## Done when
- A `jjx_record` whose file list names both sides of a staged rename emits no outside-list warning for that pair.
- A rename genuinely absent from the file list still warns.
- A unit test covers the rename-pair case.

Find the site: grep the warning text "outside file list" under the JJK/VVC Rust sources.

content.text:
Three quick responses, then I'll go dig on the one that needs it:

**1. Three triggers** (mount + groom + muster) — done, no apology owed.

**3. Order** — I'll take it: **pace 1 first, pace 2 right after, ahead of the five tweaks.** Pace 1 is the GC-racing backfill and pace 2 depends on it. Easy to rail later if that's wrong.

**2. The boundary — your instinct is right, and I think it should override the earlier "include beta."** Here's the clean way to cut it: the safe boundary is **the project, across hosts — not the host, and not a consolidation across projects.** A transcript dir's name encodes the repo's working-directory path, so "this project's chats" is trivially identifiable and enforceable. That means:
- **cerebro-alpha → yes**: same project (`rbm_alpha_recipemuster`), just made on another machine — legitimately merges into alpha's store. Cross-*host*, not cross-repo.
- **beta → no**: a different project. Alpha's JJK absorbing beta's chats is exactly the cross-repo bleed you're wary of. Beta should capture its own (via its own JJK, or a one-off backup) — alpha shouldn't reach across.

So your "pretty sure" is, I think, correct — and it revises pace 1's sources to **local-alpha + cerebro-alpha only**. The cost is that beta's history needs its own capturer, which we'd handle separately.

But the part neither of us is sure of — what a JJK *release* does to veiled / consumer-local content, and whether a committed store would survive re-delivery (your `mkdir -p` question) — I shouldn't guess at. Orienting on that now.

input.prompt:
I'm working in the rbm_alpha_recipemuster repo (Tools/jjk, Tools/vok, Tools/vvk are the relevant kits). I need to understand how JJK/VOK kits are *delivered/installed* into a consumer repo and how that delivery treats veiled and consumer-local content. Report concise findings with file:line evidence — do NOT dump whole files. Search breadth: thorough across Tools/vok, Tools/vvk, Tools/jjk, and the .claude/ skills/commands.

Answer these specific questions:

1. The delivery/install path: how does a kit (JJK especially) get emplaced into a consumer repo? Look at the VOW pipeline (tt/vow-b.Build.sh and what it invokes), the VOK forge/emplace code (likely vofe_emplace.rs, vofc_registry.rs under Tools/vok or Tools/vvk), install/uninstall scripts (vvi_install.sh / vvu_uninstall.sh), and the rbk-prep-release / cma-prep-pr skill definitions.

2. How are `vov_veiled/` directories treated during delivery — are they shipped to consumers, or excluded as proprietary/local-only? Find the rule and its evidence.

3. When a kit is re-delivered/updated into a consumer that ALREADY has it, does the process CLOBBER (wipe-and-recreate directories wholesale) or PRESERVE consumer-local content? Specifically: what happens to `.claude/jjm/` (JJK's committed gallops/officia state) and to any `vov_veiled/` content on a re-emplace? Does emplace rewrite only a managed region/marker block, or replace directories wholesale?

4. Where does consumer-local JJK runtime state live (e.g. `.claude/jjm/`), and is that path inside or outside what the delivery/emplace process touches or overwrites?

The underlying decision I'm informing: if I add a committed chat-log store under either a `vov_veiled/` path or under `.claude/jjm/`, will a future JJK re-delivery PRESERVE it or DESTROY it? End with a direct verdict: (a) does consumer-local committed data survive re-delivery, (b) which location (`vov_veiled/` vs `.claude/jjm/` vs somewhere else) is safest for a never-delete chat store, and (c) is a defensive mkdir -p needed because the store dir may be absent on a fresh clone.

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile my final report.

## Final Report: Kit Delivery, vov_veiled Treatment, and Consumer-Local State Preservation

### 1. **The Delivery/Install Path**

The kit delivery system is invoked through a two-stage bootstrap:

**Parcel Creation (Release)**: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/vok/vof/src/vofr_release.rs:82-138`
- `vofr_collect()` enumerates kit files from `Tools/{kit}/` (lines 82-138), **explicitly excluding `vov_veiled/` directories** (line 219-222)
- Kit assets are staged to a parcel directory under `kits/`
- Commands/hooks are collected from `.claude/` and staged under `claude/commands/` and `claude/hooks/`

**Consumer Installation (Emplace)**: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/vvk/vvi_install.sh:75-108`
- Bootstrap script detects platform, calls the binary: `vvx_emplace --parcel <parcel> --burc <burc.env>` (line 105)
- The Rust implementation (`vofe_emplace.rs:202-307`) performs **nuclear install**:
  - Parses `burc.env` for `BURC_TOOLS_DIR`, `BURC_PROJECT_ROOT`, `BURC_MANAGED_KITS`
  - **Lines 232-245: Deletes existing `.vvk/` and all kit directories wholesale** (`fs::remove_dir_all()`)
  - **Lines 260-279: Copies fresh kit content** from parcel, routing commands/hooks to `.claude/commands/` and `.claude/hooks/`
  - **Lines 281-285: Routes Claude config assets** from parcel `claude/` to target `.claude/`
  - Lines 289-291: Freshens `CLAUDE.md` with managed section
  - Line 295: Commits installation to git

### 2. **vov_veiled Treatment During Delivery**

**vov_veiled directories are EXCLUDED from consumer delivery — they never reach the parcel.**

**Evidence:**
- **Build/release code** (`/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/vok/vof/src/vofr_release.rs:8-9`): "Enumerate kit files, copy to staging (excludes vov_veiled/)"
- **Filtering logic** (lines 219-222): 
  ```rust
  // Skip vov_veiled/ directory
  if zvofr_is_veiled_path(rel_path) {
      continue;
  }
  ```
- **Path check** (lines 364-374): `zvofr_is_veiled_path()` returns `true` if any path component equals `"vov_veiled"`

**Consumer-facing removal** (rbk-prep-release skill, `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md:122-131`):
```
**9a. Recursive glob — all vov_veiled directories:**
git rm -rf --ignore-unmatch Tools/buk/vov_veiled/
git rm -rf --ignore-unmatch Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/
... (all kits)
```

**Verdict on vov_veiled**: Proprietary/veiled content is stripped at parcel build time and again at upstream release ceremony. Consumers never see it.

### 3. **Re-delivery Clobber vs. Preserve Behavior**

**CLOBBER — re-delivery DESTROYS existing kit directories.**

**Evidence:**
- **vofe_emplace.rs, comment at line 11**: "Emplace behavior (nuclear install):"
- **Lines 232-245**: On every emplace (including re-delivery to an existing consumer), the code **unconditionally deletes** each kit directory:
  ```rust
  for kit_id in &kit_ids {
      let kit_dir = burc.tools_dir.join(kit_id);
      if kit_dir.exists() {
          fs::remove_dir_all(&kit_dir)  // <- CLOBBER
              .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to remove kit directory {}: {}", kit_id, e))?;
      }
  }
  ```

**No selective/targeted update**: The code does not inspect what exists locally or merge content. It **wipe-and-recreate wholesale**.

**Commands/Hooks Routing** (lines 281-285, 684-704): Routes files from parcel to `.claude/commands/` and `.claude/hooks/`, using `zvofe_copy_all_files()` which does a simple file copy — not a diff-based merge.

### 4. **Consumer-Local JJK Runtime State (.claude/jjm)**

**Location**: `.claude/jjm/` relative to project root (tracked in git, see `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/jjk/README.md:119-136`)

**State of emplace logic regarding .jjm**:
- The emplace process **DOES NOT MENTION or touch `.claude/jjm/`** in any code path
- Emplace only deletes kit directories under `BURC_TOOLS_DIR` (e.g., `Tools/jjk/`) — **not `.claude/`**
- The comment at `vofe_emplace.rs:31-32` (Vacate behavior): "Kit directories are intentionally preserved (open-source utilities)" — this applies to emplace as well: only `.vvk/` is deleted, kit directories in `Tools/` are deleted, but `.claude/jjm/` is **outside the scope of the managed region**

**Git tracking of .jjm**: Files are committed to git (e.g., `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`, `.claude/jjm/current/*.md`), visible in `git ls-files .claude/jjm/ | head -10`

**Verdict on .jjm**: The emplace process does NOT touch `.claude/jjm/` at all. Consumer-local committed data in `.jjm/` **survives re-delivery** because it is not in the deletion scope.

---

## Direct Answers to Your Questions

**(a) Does consumer-local committed data survive re-delivery?**

**YES — but only for content in `.claude/jjm/` and similar "off-target" directories.**

If you store a chat log at `.claude/jjm/vov_proprietary/mychat-store.md` (committed to git), it **survives re-delivery** because:
1. Emplace only deletes kit directories under `Tools/` (lines 239-245)
2. Emplace does not delete or touch `.claude/jjm/`
3. A consumer with `git pull` sees the new commits; the local file is untouched by emplace

**However**: If you store data inside a kit directory (e.g., `Tools/jjk/mychat-store.md`), it is **DESTROYED on re-delivery** by the nuclear cleanup (line 242: `fs::remove_dir_all(&kit_dir)`).

**(b) Which location is safest for a never-delete chat store?**

**`.claude/jjm/` (or a new subdirectory under `.claude/`)** is the safest location because:
- It is **outside the VVK-managed kit region** (emplace only manages `Tools/{kit}/` and `.vvk/`)
- It is **committed to git**, so it survives clones and re-delivery
- JJK already uses `.claude/jjm/` for runtime state (see `README.md:119-136` and tracked files like `jjg_gallops.json`)
- `vov_veiled/` directories (e.g., `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/`) are **proprietary and stripped**, so they are unsuitable for consumer-committed data

**Recommendation**: Store consumer-committed chat logs under `.claude/jjm/` (or a new subdirectory like `.claude/jjm/chat-archive/` if you want to organize them separately), not under `vov_veiled/`.

**(c) Is a defensive mkdir -p needed because the store dir may be absent on a fresh clone?**

**NO — if you commit the directory structure to git.**

If you add `.claude/jjm/chat-archive/.gitkeep` (or any committed file) to the repo, the directory exists on all clones. However:
- **If the store file itself does not exist initially**, you may need `mkdir -p` when writing to it for the first time
- **If you want the directory to exist even without files**, commit a `.gitkeep` or similar marker file

Given that `.claude/jjm/` is already in git with committed files (e.g., `jji_itch.md`, `jjs_scar.md` in subdirectories), a fresh clone will have the directory structure. Your chat store, if it's a new file, will need defensive initialization on first write.

---

## Summary Table

| Aspect | Finding | Evidence |
|--------|---------|----------|
| **Delivery Path** | `vvi_install.sh` → `vvx_emplace` Rust binary | `/Tools/vvk/vvi_install.sh:105` + `/Tools/vok/vof/src/vofe_emplace.rs:202-307` |
| **vov_veiled Handling** | EXCLUDED from parcel at build time | `/Tools/vok/vof/src/vofr_release.rs:219-222` (zvofr_is_veiled_path skip) |
| **Re-delivery Strategy** | CLOBBER — nuclear deletion + fresh copy | `/Tools/vok/vof/src/vofe_emplace.rs:232-245` (fs::remove_dir_all per kit) |
| **Commands/Hooks** | Routed from parcel to `.claude/commands/` and `.claude/hooks/` | `/Tools/vok/vof/src/vofe_emplace.rs:281-285` + lines 684-704 |
| **.claude/jjm Scope** | NOT touched by emplace; survives re-delivery | No mention in vofe_emplace.rs; deletion only targets `BURC_TOOLS_DIR` |
| **Safest Store Location** | `.claude/jjm/` or subdirectory | Outside managed region; already used by JJK; tracked in git |
| **Fresh Clone Risk** | Low if directory is committed; defensive mkdir-p on first write | `.claude/jjm/` exists in git; individual files need initialization |

## Final Report: Kit Delivery, vov_veiled Treatment, and Consumer-Local State Preservation

### 1. **The Delivery/Install Path**

The kit delivery system is invoked through a two-stage bootstrap:

**Parcel Creation (Release)**: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/vok/vof/src/vofr_release.rs:82-138`

  - Parses `burc.env` for `BURC_TOOLS_DIR`, `BURC_PROJECT_ROOT`, `BURC_MANAGED_KITS`

### 2. **vov_veiled Treatment During Delivery**

**vov_veiled directories are EXCLUDED from consumer delivery — they never reach the parcel.**

**Evidence:**

  ```rust
  // Skip vov_veiled/ directory
  if zvofr_is_veiled_path(rel_path) {
      continue;
  ```

- **Path check** (lines 364-374): `zvofr_is_veiled_path()` returns `true` if any path component equals `"vov_veiled"`

**Consumer-facing removal** (rbk-prep-release skill, `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md:122-131`):
```
**9a. Recursive glob — all vov_veiled directories:**
git rm -rf --ignore-unmatch Tools/buk/vov_veiled/
git rm -rf --ignore-unmatch Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/
... (all kits)
```

**Verdict on vov_veiled**: Proprietary/veiled content is stripped at parcel build time and again at upstream release ceremony. Consumers never see it.

### 3. **Re-delivery Clobber vs. Preserve Behavior**

**CLOBBER — re-delivery DESTROYS existing kit directories.**

**Evidence:**
- **vofe_emplace.rs, comment at line 11**: "Emplace behavior (nuclear install):"
- **Lines 232-245**: On every emplace (including re-delivery to an existing consumer), the code **unconditionally deletes** each kit directory:
  ```rust
  for kit_id in &kit_ids {
      let kit_dir = burc.tools_dir.join(kit_id);
      if kit_dir.exists() {
          fs::remove_dir_all(&kit_dir)  // <- CLOBBER

  }
  ```

**No selective/targeted update**: The code does not inspect what exists locally or merge content. It **wipe-and-recreate wholesale**.

**Commands/Hooks Routing** (lines 281-285, 684-704): Routes files from parcel to `.claude/commands/` and `.claude/hooks/`, using `zvofe_copy_all_files()` which does a simple file copy — not a diff-based merge.

### 4. **Consumer-Local JJK Runtime State (.claude/jjm)**

**Location**: `.claude/jjm/` relative to project root (tracked in git, see `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/jjk/README.md:119-136`)

**State of emplace logic regarding .jjm**:

**Verdict on .jjm**: The emplace process does NOT touch `.claude/jjm/` at all. Consumer-local committed data in `.jjm/` **survives re-delivery** because it is not in the deletion scope.

**(c) Is a defensive mkdir -p needed because the store dir may be absent on a fresh clone?**

If you add `.claude/jjm/chat-archive/.gitkeep` (or any committed file) to the repo, the directory exists on all clones. However:
- **If the store file itself does not exist initially**, you may need `mkdir -p` when writing to it for the first time

Oriented — and the investigation flips one of our standing assumptions, so this is the important finding of the session.

**The store does *not* belong in `vov_veiled/`.** Delivery is a nuclear install: emplace `remove_dir_all`s each kit directory under `Tools/{kit}/` and recreates it from the parcel (`vofe_emplace.rs`), and `vov_veiled/` is *excluded* from the parcel entirely (`vofr_release.rs` skips it; `rbk-prep-release` `git rm`s it). So a chat store under `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/` would be **both absent on consumers and destroyed on any re-delivery** — exactly wrong for a never-forget store.

**The right home is `.claude/jjm/`** (a new subdir, e.g. `.claude/jjm/chat-archive/`). Emplace never touches `.claude/jjm/` — it only clobbers `Tools/{kit}/` and `.vvk/`. It's committed to git, it's already JJK's consumer-local state home (the gallops live there), and because it isn't in the parcel, it's never delivered to a consumer — so it satisfies the non-leak requirement *and* survives re-delivery. `vov_veiled/` only managed the first.

**Your cross-repo worry then resolves for free.** `.claude/jjm/` is per-repo by construction, so each repo's store holds only *its own* project's chats — there's no mechanism for alpha to absorb beta. cerebro-alpha (same project, other host) merges in; beta (a different project) is excluded structurally, not by a rule we have to remember. Your "pretty sure" was right.

Direct answers to the three things you raised:
- **Veiled dirs on release:** stripped from the parcel and wiped on re-emplace — unsafe for persistence.
- **mkdir -p:** yes, defensive on first write; commit a `.gitkeep` so the dir exists on fresh clones. (`.claude/jjm/` survives; the store *file* still needs first-write init.)
- **Cross-repo:** confirmed — capture only the current project's transcript dir; `.claude/jjm/` enforces it.

One nuance worth your knowledge, not mine: `vov_veiled/` would technically survive in a repo that *never receives a re-delivery* (a pure source). But since beta and any real consumer do receive deliveries and you want one rule, `.claude/jjm/` is the robust choice everywhere — no need to track which repos are sources.

And one loose end the boundary creates: dropping beta from alpha's backfill means **beta's history needs its own capturer** (beta's own JJK once this lands, or a one-off backup) — I'd log that as a separate itch, not fold it into these two paces.

content.text:
You've found a real gap — but I don't think the fix is moving gallops into veiled. It's that the system has *two* "never ships" categories and only one of them has a name.

**Two never-ship gestalts, not one:**

- **Veiled = kit-private *source*.** Proprietary authored content; never ships because it's IP you're not giving away. It lives under `Tools/{kit}/`, it's reviewed as design, and it's subject to the kit's lifecycle — emplace's nuclear install `remove_dir_all`s the whole kit directory and rebuilds from the parcel (which excludes veiled). Veiled is *replaceable source*.
- **jjm = project-local *state*.** Generated runtime data — your heats, officia, and now the chats. Never ships because it's *yours* and shipping it would be nonsensical (you'd be delivering your heats into a consumer's repo). And it *must* survive kit updates.

The decisive fact: **veiled is wiped on every re-delivery.** A consumer who updates JJK would lose every heat the moment gallops lived in veiled — that's not a preference, it's a hard lifecycle incompatibility. Two more mismatches stack on top: veiled is *authored source*, gallops is *generated state*; and veiled is *per-kit* (each kit has its own `vov_veiled/`), while gallops is *per-project* — one `.claude/jjm/` for the whole repo, not one per kit.

So gallops isn't in the wrong *place*. It's in the right place for a category that never got veiled's crisp articulation. Here's the murk you're actually sensing: jjm survives re-delivery today only because emplace's deletion scope happens to be narrow (`Tools/{kit}` + `.vvk`, never `.claude/`). That's an *emergent* guarantee, not a *stated* one — which is exactly why it feels murky next to veiled's clean rule.

**The clean move, if you want the symmetry: don't merge — name the second rule.** Declare the jjm-class a first-class gestalt — "project-local state under `.claude/`: release never ships it, emplace never touches it" — and it sits beside veiled with equal clarity. Gallops doesn't move, the chat store just joins it under the same principle, and emplace's hands-off-`.claude/` behavior becomes a *contract* instead of an accident. The cost is articulation (a spec line + an enforced emplace boundary), not a refactor.

And the urgent chat work stays unblocked under either outcome: pin the store to live *alongside gallops* — a relative relationship, not an absolute path — so it tracks that category wherever it lands now or later.

Where I'm least sure: whether you'd want to invest in *naming* the state-gestalt now versus just noting it and letting it mature. But I'm confident gallops shouldn't go *into* veiled — the re-delivery wipe alone settles that. What's pulling you toward veiled that the "name the second rule" framing doesn't capture?

input.content:
# Memo: Chat-Log Capture & Cost Reconstruction (JJK)

*2026-06-15. Provenance for a design session (Brad + Claude Opus 4.8). Informs the
₣BD chat-capture paces — (1) store decision + first-time backfill, (2) auto-recurring
capture — and a deferred cost-analytics pace. No source changed this session; the
working prototypes were throwaway jq+bash.*

## Why

Claude Code transcripts live at `~/.claude/projects/<cwd-encoded>/*.jsonl`. They are
machine-local and GC-pruned (a `~/.claude/.last-cleanup` runs periodically), so the
chat record is a perishable asset: you cannot analyze a conversation that has already
been silently reaped. JJK should capture these logs into the project before they
disappear; **cost and other analytics ride on the captured corpus later** — capture
first, analytics after, because capture is the part that races the clock.

## Empirical findings (this repo, measured 2026-06-15)

### Cost is reconstructable, not stored

- Transcripts store per-assistant-message **token usage + model**, never a dollar
  cost. `/usage`'s cost is *computed*: tokens × public per-token rates. (No `costUSD`
  field in current Claude Code transcripts; older versions had one — format drift.)
- `message.usage` fields: `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `cache_read_input_tokens`,
  `cache_creation_input_tokens` (+ a `cache_creation.ephemeral_1h/_5m` split), plus
  `message.model`.
- Validated: a `/usage`-reported **$6.91** session reconstructs to **$6.89** — the
  gap is rounding in the displayed token figures. Method is sound.

### Rates (per 1M tokens, list price; cache rates derived from the input rate)

| Model | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|
| opus 4.8 / 4.7 / 4.6 | $5 | $25 |
| sonnet 4.6 | $3 | $15 |
| haiku 4.5 | $1 | $5 |
| fable 5 | $10 | $50 |

Cache read = 0.1× input; cache write 5m TTL = 1.25× input; 1h TTL = 2× input (Claude
Code writes 1h). Model `<synthetic>` = $0 (locally-injected turns, not billed).

### Whole-project totals

- **~$6,839** of API-equivalent value across **63 days** (deduped). On the
  subscription plan this is the shadow price, not what was paid.
- By model: opus-4-7 dominant (~$4,089), then opus-4-8 (~$1,121), opus-4-6 (~$830),
  fable-5 (~$796), sonnet-4-6 (~$2).
- **Dedup is load-bearing.** ~43,608 of ~89,516 assistant records are forked/resumed
  copies sharing the same API `message.id` but **distinct transcript `uuid`s** (a fork
  re-stamps uuids). Dedup **by `message.id`** in the cost parser bills each generation
  once; a naive sum nearly doubles the total. Git delta handles the *storage*
  redundancy of the verbatim copies on its own.

### Byte mass (839 MB raw for this project)

| Bucket | ~MB | What |
|---|---|---|
| tool_result | 102 | file reads, grep/glob, command output |
| tool_use | 26 | tool inputs incl. Write/Edit payloads |
| other_records | 18 | per-session injected context boilerplate |
| assistant_text | 16 | the assistant's prose |
| user_text | 4 | operator messages |
| thinking | ~0 | omitted/empty on 4.8/4.7 |
| embedded images | 0 | none present |

The conversation you'd actually study (prose) ≈ **20 MB**; the rest is machinery,
most of it recoverable from git (file reads/writes) or compressible (verbatim forks).

## Cinched decisions

- **Home:** JJK / Rust (`jjx`).
- **Attribution is in-band.** Every jjx call is recorded in the transcript as a
  `tool_use` carrying its coronet/firemark + officium, interleaved with billed turns
  in timestamp order. So heat/pace attribution is a single forward pass over one
  session's transcript — no external join, concurrency-safe across multiple officia.
  (Confirmed empirically: e.g. `jjx_record ₢A6AAC` under officium `☉260413-1019`.)
- **Wholesale capture, per-project boundary.** Capture every session in the transcript
  dir matching this repo's cwd; no relevance filtering. The boundary is the
  **project, across hosts** — not the host, and not a cross-project consolidation.
  cerebro-alpha (same project, other host) merges in; **beta (a different project) is
  excluded by construction** — one repo must never absorb another's chats.
- **Never-forget but update.** Additive sync: pick up new session files and the growth
  of existing ones; never delete from the store even when the source disappears. Git
  history carries the "never forget."
- **Compression is git's own zlib + delta.** No external compressor. Supply-chain
  reasoning: a pinned/checksummed Cargo crate beats an *ambient, unpinned* system
  binary (`zstd` CLI), but git-zlib adds **zero** new trust surface and is the tightest
  exposure management — and for a rarely-read, git-delta-friendly archive the ratio
  gain of zstd did not justify the added C/FFI/build surface.
- **Triggers:** mount + groom + muster (the frequent orientation ceremonies; more
  frequent than wrap, which is rare under chat daisy-chaining).
- **Deferred:** cost accounting, attribution, analytics — later paces on the captured
  corpus.

## Storage finding (corrects a prior assumption)

The store does **not** belong in `vov_veiled/`. From the delivery-path investigation:

- `vov_veiled/` is **excluded from the parcel** (`Tools/vok/vof/src/vofr_release.rs`
  skips veiled paths; the `rbk-prep-release` ceremony `git rm`s them).
- Delivery is a **nuclear install**: `Tools/vok/vof/src/vofe_emplace.rs`
  `remove_dir_all`s each `Tools/{kit}/` and rebuilds from the parcel. So a store under
  `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/` would be **both absent on consumers and destroyed on
  re-delivery**.
- `.claude/jjm/` is the right home: emplace only touches `Tools/{kit}` + `.vvk`, never
  `.claude/`; jjm is committed to git and consumer-local (not in the parcel → not
  distributed). The store lives **alongside gallops**, e.g. `.claude/jjm/chat-archive/`.
- Defensive `mkdir -p` on first write; commit a `.gitkeep` so the dir exists on fresh
  clones.

## Open thread: the state gestalt (not resolved)

There are **two "never ships" categories, and only one is named:**

- **veiled** = kit-private *source* — proprietary authored content under `Tools/{kit}/`,
  reviewed as design, *replaceable* (wiped + rebuilt on re-emplace).
- **jjm** = project-local *state* — generated runtime data under `.claude/`, must
  *survive* updates, scoped *per-project* not per-kit.

jjm survives re-delivery today only because emplace's deletion scope happens to be
narrow — an **emergent** guarantee, not a **stated** one. That is the "murkiness"
next to veiled's clean rule. Open question: name the state-gestalt as a first-class
rule (release never ships it, emplace never touches it) so jjm's protection becomes a
contract parallel to veiled's. **Gallops should not move into veiled** — lifecycle
(wiped on re-emplace), category (source vs state), and scope (per-kit vs per-project)
all mismatch. Discussion ongoing.

## Follow-ups

- **Two capture paces drafted, not yet slated** into ₣BD: (1) decide store +
  first-time multi-source backfill (local-alpha + cerebro-alpha); (2) auto-recurring
  capture at mount/groom/muster. Order: (1) first. Store pinned "alongside gallops" so
  it tracks that category regardless of the gestalt outcome.
- **Beta's history needs its own capturer** (beta's own JJK once this lands, or a
  one-off backup) — alpha must not reach across.
- **Cost-analytics pace** deferred until the corpus is captured.
- **State-gestalt naming** — open (above).

## Prototype residue

Throwaway jq + bash prototypes from this session lived in `/tmp` (`/tmp/rbcost.jq`,
`/tmp/breakdown.jq`) — proof-of-method only, never repo artifacts. The real
implementation is Rust in `jjx`.

# Memo: Chat-Log Capture & Cost Reconstruction (JJK)

*2026-06-15. Provenance for a design session (Brad + Claude Opus 4.8). Informs the
₣BD chat-capture paces — (1) store decision + first-time backfill, (2) auto-recurring
capture — and a deferred cost-analytics pace. No source changed this session; the
working prototypes were throwaway jq+bash.*

## Why

Claude Code transcripts live at `~/.claude/projects/<cwd-encoded>/*.jsonl`. They are
machine-local and GC-pruned (a `~/.claude/.last-cleanup` runs periodically), so the
chat record is a perishable asset: you cannot analyze a conversation that has already
been silently reaped. JJK should capture these logs into the project before they
disappear; **cost and other analytics ride on the captured corpus later** — capture
first, analytics after, because capture is the part that races the clock.

## Empirical findings (this repo, measured 2026-06-15)

### Cost is reconstructable, not stored

- Transcripts store per-assistant-message **token usage + model**, never a dollar
  cost. `/usage`'s cost is *computed*: tokens × public per-token rates. (No `costUSD`
  field in current Claude Code transcripts; older versions had one — format drift.)
- `message.usage` fields: `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `cache_read_input_tokens`,
  `cache_creation_input_tokens` (+ a `cache_creation.ephemeral_1h/_5m` split), plus
  `message.model`.
- Validated: a `/usage`-reported **$6.91** session reconstructs to **$6.89** — the
  gap is rounding in the displayed token figures. Method is sound.

### Rates (per 1M tokens, list price; cache rates derived from the input rate)

| Model | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|
| opus 4.8 / 4.7 / 4.6 | $5 | $25 |
| sonnet 4.6 | $3 | $15 |
| haiku 4.5 | $1 | $5 |
| fable 5 | $10 | $50 |

Cache read = 0.1× input; cache write 5m TTL = 1.25× input; 1h TTL = 2× input (Claude
Code writes 1h). Model `<synthetic>` = $0 (locally-injected turns, not billed).

### Whole-project totals

- **~$6,839** of API-equivalent value across **63 days** (deduped). On the
  subscription plan this is the shadow price, not what was paid.
- By model: opus-4-7 dominant (~$4,089), then opus-4-8 (~$1,121), opus-4-6 (~$830),
  fable-5 (~$796), sonnet-4-6 (~$2).
- **Dedup is load-bearing.** ~43,608 of ~89,516 assistant records are forked/resumed
  copies sharing the same API `message.id` but **distinct transcript `uuid`s** (a fork
  re-stamps uuids). Dedup **by `message.id`** in the cost parser bills each generation
  once; a naive sum nearly doubles the total. Git delta handles the *storage*
  redundancy of the verbatim copies on its own.

### Byte mass (839 MB raw for this project)

| Bucket | ~MB | What |
|---|---|---|
| tool_result | 102 | file reads, grep/glob, command output |
| tool_use | 26 | tool inputs incl. Write/Edit payloads |
| other_records | 18 | per-session injected context boilerplate |
| assistant_text | 16 | the assistant's prose |
| user_text | 4 | operator messages |
| thinking | ~0 | omitted/empty on 4.8/4.7 |
| embedded images | 0 | none present |

The conversation you'd actually study (prose) ≈ **20 MB**; the rest is machinery,
most of it recoverable from git (file reads/writes) or compressible (verbatim forks).

## Cinched decisions

- **Home:** JJK / Rust (`jjx`).
- **Attribution is in-band.** Every jjx call is recorded in the transcript as a
  `tool_use` carrying its coronet/firemark + officium, interleaved with billed turns
  in timestamp order. So heat/pace attribution is a single forward pass over one
  session's transcript — no external join, concurrency-safe across multiple officia.
  (Confirmed empirically: e.g. `jjx_record ₢A6AAC` under officium `☉260413-1019`.)
- **Wholesale capture, per-project boundary.** Capture every session in the transcript
  dir matching this repo's cwd; no relevance filtering. The boundary is the
  **project, across hosts** — not the host, and not a cross-project consolidation.
  cerebro-alpha (same project, other host) merges in; **beta (a different project) is
  excluded by construction** — one repo must never absorb another's chats.
- **Never-forget but update.** Additive sync: pick up new session files and the growth
  of existing ones; never delete from the store even when the source disappears. Git
  history carries the "never forget."
- **Compression is git's own zlib + delta.** No external compressor. Supply-chain
  reasoning: a pinned/checksummed Cargo crate beats an *ambient, unpinned* system
  binary (`zstd` CLI), but git-zlib adds **zero** new trust surface and is the tightest
  exposure management — and for a rarely-read, git-delta-friendly archive the ratio
  gain of zstd did not justify the added C/FFI/build surface.
- **Triggers:** mount + groom + muster (the frequent orientation ceremonies; more
  frequent than wrap, which is rare under chat daisy-chaining).
- **Deferred:** cost accounting, attribution, analytics — later paces on the captured
  corpus.

## Storage finding (corrects a prior assumption)

The store does **not** belong in `vov_veiled/`. From the delivery-path investigation:

- `vov_veiled/` is **excluded from the parcel** (`Tools/vok/vof/src/vofr_release.rs`
  skips veiled paths; the `rbk-prep-release` ceremony `git rm`s them).
- Delivery is a **nuclear install**: `Tools/vok/vof/src/vofe_emplace.rs`
  `remove_dir_all`s each `Tools/{kit}/` and rebuilds from the parcel. So a store under
  `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/` would be **both absent on consumers and destroyed on
  re-delivery**.
- `.claude/jjm/` is the right home: emplace only touches `Tools/{kit}` + `.vvk`, never
  `.claude/`; jjm is committed to git and consumer-local (not in the parcel → not
  distributed). The store lives **alongside gallops**, e.g. `.claude/jjm/chat-archive/`.
- Defensive `mkdir -p` on first write; commit a `.gitkeep` so the dir exists on fresh
  clones.

## Open thread: the state gestalt (not resolved)

There are **two "never ships" categories, and only one is named:**

- **veiled** = kit-private *source* — proprietary authored content under `Tools/{kit}/`,
  reviewed as design, *replaceable* (wiped + rebuilt on re-emplace).
- **jjm** = project-local *state* — generated runtime data under `.claude/`, must
  *survive* updates, scoped *per-project* not per-kit.

jjm survives re-delivery today only because emplace's deletion scope happens to be
narrow — an **emergent** guarantee, not a **stated** one. That is the "murkiness"
next to veiled's clean rule. Open question: name the state-gestalt as a first-class
rule (release never ships it, emplace never touches it) so jjm's protection becomes a
contract parallel to veiled's. **Gallops should not move into veiled** — lifecycle
(wiped on re-emplace), category (source vs state), and scope (per-kit vs per-project)
all mismatch. Discussion ongoing.

- **Two capture paces drafted, not yet slated** into ₣BD: (1) decide store +

33	
34	### B. Foray Protocol — Fundus constants (rbm values)
35	
36	**Fundus constants** (use these for `jjx_bind`):
37	- Default reldir: `projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster`
38	- BURN alias: use the alias from the target's BUK Regime Node profile (e.g., `winhost-wsl`, `winhost-cyg`)
39	
40	Hosts in play for this repo:
41	- `winhost-wsl` / `winhost-cyg` — Windows host transports (WSL and Cygwin).
42	- `cerebro` — Linux test host used as a fundus by JJK fundus scenario tests.
43	
44	In the public Foray Workflow, the bind example reldir is genericized to
45	`<your-project-reldir>`; the rbm-specific value is `projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster`.
46	
47	### C. Build & Run Discipline (rbm/VOK-specific)
48	
49	**Build & Run Discipline:**
50	Always run these after Rust code changes:
51	- `tt/vow-b.Build.sh` — Build
52	- `tt/vvw-r.RunVVX.sh` — Run VVX
53	
54	### D. Gradient delivery — mount and groom
55	
56	Governs the Mount and Groom protocols in `claude-jjk-core.md`: when those
57	protocols say to surface the docket, approach, or paddock, this is *how* to
58	surface them.
59	
60	Mount and groom surface heat artifacts (paddock, docket) written AI-for-AI and
61	dense by default; the operator does not read dockets directly and may be tired.
62	This is a *craft constraint on presentation, not a cap on thinking* — hold the
63	full analysis internally; shape the delivery so the operator has a place to
64	stand and a way to climb. Default, not gated on how long ago the pace was
65	slated: a same-session AI-dense docket is already a wall by afternoon.
66	
67	At mount and groom:
68	
69	- **Lead with one sentence the operator can stand on** — the pace goal (mount)
70	  or heat state (groom) — then *stop and let them pull the rest down*. Do not
71	  follow the opening sentence with the full docket, the approach, and every
72	  flagged concern in one turn. That sequence is the wall.
73	- **One path fully; alternatives in a line.** Present the single recommended
74	  path in full; give each alternative one line the operator can ask you to
75	  expand. Three fully-detailed options is word-wall in the costume of
76	  thoroughness.
77	- **Depth on request, in layers.** The operator throttles — "more," "the
78	  detail," "the others" pulls the next layer.
79	
80	Shared antipattern names (use as shorthand, either direction) — concept homes
81	are MCM's Antipatterns catalog; this section carries only the avoidance moves:
82	
83	- **Stun** (MCM `mcm_stun`) — the wall-of-prose delivery defect. Avoidance is
84	  the protocol above: one sentence to stand on, one path fully, depth in
85	  layers.
86	- **Molehill** (MCM `mcm_molehill`) — the manufactured decision-fork. Before
87	  presenting a decision or concern, weigh its real stakes; if small, treat it
88	  in a clause, not a fork. Unsure if it's a molehill? Say so in a line rather
89	  than building it up.
90	
91	**The trot** — the Stun-recovery delivery pattern (as a horse is trotted out
92	in hand for inspection: deliberate pace, one animal at a time, the examiner
93	setting the rhythm). A dense deliverable is broken into a pre-counted sequence
94	of small chunks ("chunk 2 of 6"); each chunk opens with a plain-language
95	context reminder before its content; the operator paces the advance ("next"),
96	and the trot absorbs detours — a stray thought mid-walk spawns its own
97	discussion, then the walk resumes where it stood. A chunk often leans on work the operator did not author — a
98	prior instance's, or a session they convened but did not line-read; there the
99	context reminder grounds the referenced artifact *from scratch* rather than
100	invoking it by name, because the operator's strategy of building across editions
101	holds them, by design, at a remove from the words, so a name that feels shared to
102	the trotter ("the normative-register clause," "the blessed form") frequently
103	names nothing they have read. The pull to present such work *for ratification* is
104	the tell that review is being presumed where it is not — the cue to unpack before
105	being asked, not after. Distinct from gradient
106	delivery above, which layers depth on *one* deliverable for the operator to
107	pull: a trot serializes a *multi-part* deliverable, re-grounding at every
108	step for a tired reader. Use it both directions — the operator can ask to
109	"trot the findings," and the agent should offer a trot when it sees a dense
110	multi-part response coming or the operator reports fatigue.

59	
60	When generating heredocs for stdin content, the delimiter must not appear alone on any line within the content.

63	- **Safe alternatives**: `SPEC`, `CONTENT`, `DOC`, `PACESPEC`, `SLASHCMD`

69	  - Attribute reference: `:prefix_snake_case:` (mapping section)

71	  - Definition: `[[anchor]] {attribute}:: Definition text` (meaning)
72	- Definitions may be grouped in lists or dispersed through document
73	- Maintain consistent prefix categories (e.g., `mcm_`, `rbw_`, `gad_`)
74	- Use snake_case for anchors, match attribute to anchor

100	| `complete` | `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.complete.sh` | All of the above | service ∪ crucible (21 fixtures) |

Its operator-local cases (station/oauth/auth/node/privilege) self-skip when the regime is not configured on the machine.

# jjezs_slate chat-capture-store-and-backfill

## Character
Decide-then-do, but mostly do.
The store location is settled (alongside gallops); the work is a one-time, multi-source backfill.
The backfill can be scripted by hand — only the recurring mechanism (next pace) needs a Rust hook.

## Goal
Perform the first-time backfill of the full Claude Code transcript history into a committed store,
wholesale, before Claude Code's GC prunes it.

## Sources
- Local alpha — this repo's transcript dir under the user's Claude Code projects.
- Cerebro — the alpha project's transcripts on the cerebro host, alpha only, gathered over ssh;
  the same project on another host, so it merges into the same store.

## Cinched
- The store lives alongside gallops under `.claude/jjm/` (a new subdir), never in `vov_veiled/`.
  Rationale: emplace nuclear-wipes kit directories and the parcel excludes veiled, so a veiled store
  would be both undistributed and destroyed on re-delivery, whereas `.claude/jjm/` is untouched by
  emplace, committed, and consumer-local.
  Provenance: `Memos/memo-20260615-chat-capture-and-cost-reconstruction.md`.
- Wholesale — every session in the project's transcript dir, no relevance filtering.
- Per-project boundary — capture only the transcript dir matching this repo's working directory;
  never absorb another repo's chats. Beta is out of scope; it gets its own capturer.
- Compression is git's own zlib + delta; no external compressor, no new dependency.
- The commit is scoped to the store paths, orthogonal to other officia's work.

## Mount-time decisions
- Exact subdir name and per-session file layout (one file per source `.jsonl` is the natural shape);
  a committed `.gitkeep` so the dir survives fresh clones; `mkdir -p` on first write.
- The "name the state-gestalt" question (see memo) is open but does not block:
  the store is pinned alongside gallops and rides that category wherever it later lands.

## Done when
- The store exists under `.claude/jjm/` and the full current transcript history from local-alpha and
  cerebro-alpha is committed into it.
- The commit is scoped to store paths.

# jjezs_slate chat-capture-auto-recurring

## Character
The recurring capture mechanism in jjx (Rust), built on the store the prior pace establishes.
Idempotent and additive.

## Goal
Automatically augment the chat-log store on the frequent orientation ceremonies,
so new sessions and the growth of existing ones are captured with no manual action —
never forgetting, only adding.

## Cinched
- Triggers: mount, groom, and muster.
- Never-forget but update — additive sync: pick up new session files and the growth of existing ones;
  never delete from the store even when the source disappears (git history carries never-forget).
- Idempotent — an unchanged run is a no-op, no empty commit.
- Wholesale, per-project boundary (the transcript dir matching this repo's working directory);
  git's own compression; the `.claude/jjm/` store the prior pace establishes.
- Recurring capture is local-only — cerebro-alpha is swept by the backfill pace and periodic manual
  runs, not on every ceremony (an ssh pull per muster is too heavy).

## Mount-time decisions
- The commit: do the (today read-only) mount/groom/muster handlers gain a scoped capture commit,
  or stage-and-defer? A commit scoped to store paths matches the orthogonal-commit discipline.
- Change-detection for idempotency (size+mtime vs content hash).
- Coordinate with the mount/groom firemark-from-gazette rework already enrolled in ₣BD —
  both add work to the same ceremonies (coordinate, don't block).

## Done when
- A mount, groom, or muster commits any new or grown local sessions to the store;
  an unchanged run is a no-op.
- All three ceremonies carry the hook.

```
veiled is stripped from the parcel). Before copying, confirm the exact subdir name and layout with
me: one file per source .jsonl, namespaced by host so local and cerebro can't collide, a committed
.gitkeep, and mkdir -p on first write.

no zstd or external compressor). This is a large commit (~hundreds of MB), so confirm the layout with
me first, then commit scoped to the store paths only via jjx_record with an explicit file list
affiliated to ₢BDAAX — not wrap.
```

That chat will land on the docket and memo for the full picture, settle the layout with you, and do the copy + scoped commit. When it's done, ₢BDAAY (the recurring hook) is the next pace whenever you want to build it.

You *did* say "include both alpha and beta directories" early on. Later you raised the cross-repo worry **tentatively** — "I'm pretty sure (but not certain)" — and I hardened that musing into a firm "drop beta," then carried it straight into the docket, the memo, and the prompt as if it were settled.
